question: Describe the normal heating and critical limit pressure-temperature curve.

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: The System Evaluation section describes how the NuScale Power Plant optimizes performance across various power levels by establishing steady-state parameters such as coolant temperatures, flow rates, and pressures, using both software modeling and hand calculations. The evaluation considers uncertainties in flow resistance and thermal head, ensuring efficient operation, safety margins, and minimal pressure losses to support the plant’s natural circulation design.

nuscale_ch05_0018.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0018.summary: This section outlines the requirements and procedures for inspection, shipment, installation, operating conditions, inservice surveillance, and threaded fasteners for the NuScale reactor vessel, referencing relevant ASME and ASTM standards. It emphasizes compliance with specific codes for packaging, handling, and quality assurance, as well as maintaining a controlled environment during shipment to ensure vessel integrity. Key references to industry standards are also provided.

nuscale_ch05_0016.title: Pressure-Temperature Limits, Pressurized Thermal Shock, and Charpy Upper-Shelf Energy Data and Analyses
nuscale_ch05_0016.summary: This section outlines the methodology and regulatory compliance for establishing pressure-temperature limits for the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) over the plant’s 60-year life. It describes how these limits are calculated using ASME and NRC guidelines, accounting for vessel embrittlement, and provides the relevant curves and data to ensure protection against non-ductile failure.

nuscale_ch05_0024.title: Pressurizer
nuscale_ch05_0024.summary: The pressurizer, integrated into the upper region of the reactor vessel and separated by a baffle plate, regulates Reactor Coolant System (RCS) pressure by maintaining a saturated steam-water interface. It uses heaters and spray flow to control pressure and is designed to handle system transients without triggering reactor trips or safety system actuations, with instrumentation providing continuous monitoring and control.

nuscale_ch05_0015.title: Reactor Vessel Materials
nuscale_ch05_0015.summary: The NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV) is a key component of the reactor coolant system, housing the core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a containment vessel for safety. The RPV and its materials are selected and fabricated according to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure long-term integrity and prevent the release of fission products. Material specifications for all components, including bolting and welds, are detailed in referenced tables and comply with strict regulatory requirements.

edges.description: Section A discusses the establishment of steady state values for primary and secondary side parameters as a function of reactor power, which are necessary for defining the normal heating and pressure-temperature behavior, while Section B details inspection and handling requirements for the reactor vessel, which are prerequisites to ensuring the vessel's integrity before such operational evaluations can be meaningful.

Section A discusses the establishment of steady state pressure and temperature values for optimal reactor performance, which are directly relevant to the pressure-temperature limits and analyses described in Section B.

Section A discusses the establishment of steady state pressure and temperature values for optimal system performance, while Section B describes the pressurizer's role in maintaining and communicating pressure within the reactor coolant system, directly affecting the pressure-temperature relationship relevant to the critical limit curve.

Section A discusses the establishment of steady state pressure and temperature values for optimal system performance, which are directly relevant to the pressure-temperature limits that must be maintained to protect the reactor vessel materials described in Section B.

Section A outlines inspection and handling requirements for the reactor pressure vessel, while Section B discusses pressure-temperature limits and material properties, both of which are connected through their reliance on ASME BPVC code compliance for ensuring vessel integrity under operational and thermal conditions.

Section A describes inspection, shipment, and storage requirements for the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which structurally includes the pressurizer described in Section B as an integral part of the RPV, thus connecting the physical and regulatory context of both sections.

Section A references the inspection, shipment, and handling requirements for the reactor pressure vessel, while Section B provides a general description of the reactor vessel's structure and function, with Section A specifying procedural details for the general component described in Section B.

Section A discusses pressure-temperature limits and material requirements for the reactor vessel, while Section B describes the pressurizer's design and its role in controlling reactor pressure, both of which are directly connected through their influence on the pressure-temperature curve relevant to normal heating and critical limits.

Section A discusses specific pressure-temperature limits and examination requirements for reactor vessel attachments, while Section B provides a general description of the reactor vessel and its role as a pressure-retaining component, thus Section A specifies details for the general context provided in Section B.

Section A describes the pressurizer's design and function as part of the reactor vessel, while Section B describes the reactor vessel materials and structure, with both sections providing foundational information necessary to understand the pressure-temperature behavior and limits of the system.
